title: panicum benjamini
intro: Panicum benjamini, also known as Benjamins' grass or Panicum, is a perennial grass native to North America, commonly found growing in the prairies of the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Panicum benjamini include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
